name: a11y-audit
description: Comprehensive accessibility audit — automated axe scan, keyboard navigation check, color contrast review, ARIA audit, and prioritized CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM issue backlog

name: add-observability
description: Add production observability to a service — structured logging, metrics endpoint, health checks, and Sentry error tracking. Detects project type and adds the right libraries and middleware.

name: agent-design
description: Guided workshop for designing a multi-agent system — task decomposition, agent roles, orchestration pattern, pseudocode, and failure handling strategy

name: agent-evolution
description: Review high-confidence instincts and promote them to agent-specific overlay files (~/.clarc/agent-instincts/). Closes the continuous-learning-v2 feedback loop by making agents smarter over time.

name: agent-instincts
description: Show the learned instinct overlay for a specific agent, or list all overlays with instinct counts.
whenToUse: - Before running /agent-evolution — check what is already promoted - After a session that corrected an agent — verify the correction was captured - Debugging: agent behaving unexpectedly — check if a bad instinct was promoted

name: agent-audit
description: Review a clarc agent file for quality across 8 dimensions — instruction clarity, model appropriateness, tool coverage, trigger precision, exit criteria, examples, overlap detection, and safety guardrails. Produces a scored JSON report. Use /agent-audit --all to review all agents at once.

name: agent-stats
description: Show agent usage statistics — top agents, heatmap, outcome ratio

name: analyze-feedback
description: Analyze raw user feedback (support tickets, NPS comments, app store reviews, survey responses) to identify top pain points, cluster themes, and generate idea seeds. Delegates to the feedback-analyst agent.

name: android-review
description: Android code review — Compose best practices, Hilt scoping, Room migrations, ViewModel/UiState patterns, Coroutine Dispatchers. Invokes the android-reviewer agent.

name: arc42
description: Generate or update arc42 architecture documentation with C4 diagrams for the current project. Reads the codebase and produces docs/architecture/arc42.md with PlantUML diagrams. Supports targeted section updates.

name: backstage-setup
description: Install, configure, and deploy Backstage — from npx create-app to production with GitHub integration, TechDocs, and Docker Compose local dev

name: bash-review
description: Bash/shell script review for safety, correctness, shellcheck compliance, and idiomatic style. Invokes the bash-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: - After writing or modifying shell scripts - Before committing CI/CD scripts or setup scripts - Reviewing scripts that run in production or CI environments

name: brainstorm
description: Generate product ideas from a problem space using structured ideation frameworks (JTBD, How Might We, analogy thinking, constraint reversal). Produces concrete idea seeds, not vague concepts.

name: brand-identity
description: Develop a visual brand identity for a product — generates color palette (with WCAG validation), typeface pairings, brand voice, mood board direction, and a starter Brand Guidelines structure.

name: breakdown
description: Break down a feature, epic, or initiative into sprint-ready tasks. Produces user stories, acceptance criteria, story point estimates, and a prioritized task list ready to paste into Jira, Linear, or GitHub Issues.

name: build-fix
description: Incrementally fix build and type errors with minimal, safe changes.

name: c-review
description: C code review for memory safety, C11/C17 idioms, opaque pointer patterns, and error handling. Invokes the c-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: - After writing or modifying C code - Before committing C changes - Reviewing systems or embedded code

name: cfp
description: Write a conference abstract (CFP submission) — generates abstract in 200/300/500-word versions, title variants, and speaker bio in 50/150-word versions.

name: chaos-experiment
description: Design and run a chaos experiment — from hypothesis to tool-specific commands and experiment protocol

name: chart-review
description: Review data visualizations for chart type appropriateness, WCAG accessibility (color contrast, ARIA), responsive behavior, and performance. Applies data-visualization skill.

name: checkpoint
description: Create or verify a workflow checkpoint — create, verify, list, and restore git-backed checkpoints with Memory Bank integration.

name: clarc-way
description: Interactive guide to The clarc Way methodology. Identifies your task type, recommends the right pipeline stage, and shows which commands and agents to use — with concrete examples for your current language.

name: claw
description: Start the NanoClaw agent REPL — a persistent, session-aware AI assistant powered by the claude CLI.

name: cli-review
description: Review a CLI tool implementation for ergonomics, error handling, exit codes, help text quality, and Unix composability. Applies cli-patterns and cli-ux skills.

name: code-review
description: Comprehensive code review — delegates to code-reviewer agent which routes to language specialists (typescript, go, python, java, swift, rust, cpp, ruby, elixir).

name: command-audit
description: Audit the clarc command system for UX ergonomics — naming consistency, argument design, feedback loops, step count, agent invocation, overlap detection, missing commands, and built-in conflicts. Produces a tabular overview and issue list. Use /command-audit --all for a full system audit.

name: competitive-review
description: Compare clarc against competing AI engineering workflow systems (Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, Aider, Devin, Continue.dev) — feature matrix, clarc-unique capabilities, and prioritized competitive gaps. Produces a strategic analysis report.

name: context
description: Analyze the current project stack and surface the most relevant clarc skills, agents, and commands for this codebase.

name: contract-test
description: Set up consumer-driven contract testing with Pact — write consumer pact, configure Pact Broker, set up provider verification, and add can-i-deploy gate to CI

name: cpp-build
description: Build C++ code with CMake or Make and run static analysis. Catches compilation errors, warnings, and cppcheck issues.
whenToUse: - When CMake or Make build fails - When cppcheck reports issues - After pulling changes that break compilation

name: cpp-review
description: Comprehensive C++ code review for C++ Core Guidelines, memory safety, RAII, modern C++20/23, concurrency, and performance. Invokes the cpp-reviewer agent.

name: csharp-review
description: C# 12 / .NET 8 code review for nullable reference types, async/await, CQRS patterns, and security. Invokes the csharp-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: - After writing or modifying C# code - Before committing .NET changes - Reviewing pull requests with C# code

name: cursor-setup
description: Set up Cursor IDE integration for clarc — creates .cursor/rules/ files for all installed languages and generates .cursorrules from common rules.

name: dark-mode-audit
description: Audit a codebase's dark mode implementation — checks color strategy (not just CSS invert), elevation system, token structure, contrast ratios on dark surfaces, and common mistakes. Outputs a prioritized finding list with fixes.

name: data-mesh-review
description: Data Mesh architecture review — domain boundaries, data contracts, quality checks (Great Expectations/Soda), catalog registration, SLO definitions, PII governance, and cross-domain lineage.
whenToUse: Use /data-mesh-review when: - Onboarding a new domain team to the data mesh - Reviewing a new or updated data product before it goes live

name: database-review
description: PostgreSQL schema, query, and security review — indexes, RLS, schema design, anti-patterns. Invokes the database-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: Use /database-review when: - Writing or modifying SQL queries - Creating or altering database migrations

name: debt-audit
description: Systematic technical debt inventory — complexity analysis, hotspot identification (Churn×Complexity), dependency age, test coverage gaps, and prioritized debt backlog

name: dep-audit
description: Full dependency audit — vulnerability scanning, license compliance, supply chain risk, and prioritized remediation report

name: deps
description: Dependency audit and upgrade workflow — check for outdated packages, security vulnerabilities, and safely upgrade dependencies with verification. Supports npm, pnpm, yarn, bun, pip, poetry, go mod, and cargo.

name: design-critique
description: Get structured design critique for a visual design — from a screenshot path, URL description, or code. Invokes the design-critic agent.

name: devex-survey
description: Design a Developer Experience survey — DX Core 24 questions, SPACE-aligned dimensions, Likert scale, anonymization, and action plan from results

name: discover
description: Discover product opportunities through market research. Searches for competitor features, user pain points on Reddit/HN, tech trends, and market gaps in a given domain. Generates idea seeds from what it finds.

name: docs-review
description: Review API documentation for completeness, quality, playground, code examples, changelog, navigation, and CI — produces a structured audit report with prioritised fixes.

name: doctor
description: Health-check for your clarc installation. Verifies agents, skills, commands, hooks, symlinks, MEMORY.md size, and available updates. Runs scripts/doctor.js.

name: dora-baseline
description: Measure DORA Four Keys baseline for a team — deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, MTTR — with benchmark comparison and improvement priorities

name: e2e
description: Generate and run end-to-end tests with Playwright. Creates test journeys, runs tests, captures screenshots/videos/traces, and uploads artifacts.
whenToUse: Use /e2e when: - Testing critical user journeys (login, trading, payments) - Verifying multi-step flows work end-to-end

name: eda-review
description: Review event-driven code for idempotency, ordering guarantees, schema evolution compatibility, DLQ handling, and observability — produces prioritized findings

name: edge-review
description: Review Edge Function code for runtime compatibility, bundle size, caching opportunities, and security — supports Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge, and Deno Deploy

name: elixir-review
description: Comprehensive Elixir/Phoenix code review for OTP patterns, Ecto queries, security (Sobelow), and idiomatic functional Elixir. Invokes the elixir-reviewer agent.

name: engineering-review
description: Monthly engineering health review — DORA trends, leading indicators, tech debt movement, DevEx pulse, and action items for next month

name: eval
description: Manage eval-driven development workflow — define, run, check results, and list evals for a feature. Supports eval-first development where behaviour is specified before implementation.

name: evaluate
description: Evaluate a product idea before committing to build it. Delegates to the product-evaluator agent to assess problem clarity, user fit, feasibility, alternatives, and opportunity cost. Produces a structured Go/No-Go recommendation.

name: event-storming
description: Facilitate an EventStorming workshop — Big Picture discovery, Process Level modeling, Design Level with aggregates and bounded contexts, producing a Domain Event Glossar and aggregate map

name: evolve
description: Cluster accumulated instincts into skills, commands, or agents — runs conflict detection first, groups instincts by trigger domain, scores clusters by confidence, and writes evolved files with --generate.

name: experiment
description: Design a statistically valid A/B test. Walk through hypothesis, metric selection, sample size calculation, assignment strategy, and analysis plan. Outputs a ready-to-use experiment spec.

name: explore
description: Design solutions for a validated product idea — generates 2-4 concrete approaches with trade-off analysis and an Architecture Decision Record. Use after /evaluate returns Go. Delegates to the solution-designer agent.

name: find-skill
description: Search all available skills by keyword and return the top matches with their descriptions. Use when unsure which skill covers a topic.

name: finops-audit
description: Audit cloud costs for a project — tagging coverage, rightsizing opportunities, savings plan potential, anomalies, and Infracost integration gaps

name: flutter-review
description: Flutter/Dart code review for widget architecture, performance, null safety, and accessibility. Invokes the flutter-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: - After writing or modifying Flutter/Dart code - Before committing widget or state management changes - Reviewing pull requests with Flutter code

name: frontend-arch-review
description: Micro-Frontend architecture review — team boundaries, Module Federation config, shared dependencies, routing, state sharing, fallbacks, design system consistency, and CI/CD independence.
whenToUse: Use /frontend-arch-review when: - Setting up a new Micro-Frontend architecture - Adding a new Remote app to an existing Shell

name: gitops-review
description: Review GitOps configuration for declarative correctness, sync strategy, secrets management, and progressive delivery readiness

name: go-build
description: Fix Go build errors, go vet warnings, and linter issues incrementally. Invokes the go-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
whenToUse: Use /go-build when: - go build ./... fails with errors - go vet ./... reports issues

name: go-review
description: Comprehensive Go code review for idiomatic patterns, concurrency safety, error handling, and security. Invokes the go-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: Use /go-review when: - After writing or modifying Go code - Before committing Go changes

name: go-test
description: Enforce TDD workflow for Go. Write table-driven tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with go test -cover.
whenToUse: Use /go-test when: - Implementing new Go functions - Adding test coverage to existing code

name: golden-path
description: Create a Golden Path Backstage Scaffolder template for a standard service type — generates template.yaml, skeleton files, CI/CD, and catalog-info.yaml

name: guide
description: Get a step-by-step workflow plan for a specific task using clarc components. Usage: /guide <task> — e.g. /guide add auth, /guide write E2E tests, /guide deploy to k8s

name: hook-audit
description: Audit the clarc hook system for coverage, reliability, and performance — event coverage, false positive risk, performance impact, error handling, dead references, dead hooks, missing critical hooks, and interaction conflicts. Analyzes hooks/hooks.json and scripts/hooks/*.js.

name: hooks-config
description: Configure clarc workflow hooks — enable/disable nudges, set cooldowns, view hook status. Creates/updates .clarc/hooks-config.json.

name: hooks-stats
description: Show hook invocation statistics, performance metrics, and error rates from the hook log.

name: i18n-audit
description: Audit a codebase for i18n completeness — find hardcoded user-visible strings, missing translation keys, RTL-incompatible CSS, and locale-sensitive formatting without Intl API.

name: iac-review
description: IaC code review for Pulumi and AWS CDK — abstractions, security hardening, tagging, unit tests, multi-environment separation, drift detection, and compliance policy coverage.
whenToUse: Use /iac-review when: - Adding new Pulumi programs or CDK stacks to the codebase - Writing new ComponentResources or L3 Constructs

name: icon-system
description: Generate an icon system specification — library selection recommendation, token definitions (size/stroke/color), naming convention, accessibility rules, and a React icon component template. Use when starting a new project or auditing existing icons.

name: idea
description: Capture and structure a raw product idea. Asks clarifying questions to fill gaps, then writes a structured idea document. First step in the product lifecycle before evaluation and solution design.

name: idp-design
description: Design an Internal Developer Platform (IDP) for an engineering organization — guided discovery, component prioritization, make-vs-buy decision, and 90-day roadmap

name: incident
description: Open and manage a production incident. Creates a structured incident document, guides root cause analysis, generates status page updates and Slack messages, and sets up a post-mortem after resolution.

name: instinct-export
description: Export instincts from project/global scope to a file

name: instinct-import
description: Import instincts from file or URL into project/global scope

name: instinct-outcome
description: Record an outcome (good/bad/neutral) for a specific instinct to adjust its confidence score

name: instinct-projects
description: List registered projects and their instinct/observation counts — part of the continuous-learning-v2 system.

name: instinct-report
description: Ranked list of instincts by confidence with trend indicators (↑↓→) and decay candidates

name: instinct-status
description: Show learned instincts (project + global) with confidence

name: instrument
description: Add product analytics event tracking to an existing feature or file. Designs event taxonomy, chooses the right analytics call (track/identify/group), and instruments the code without breaking existing logic.

name: java-review
description: Comprehensive Java code review for idiomatic patterns, Spring Boot 4, hexagonal architecture, DDD, JPA, and security. Invokes the java-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: Use /java-review when: - After writing or modifying Java/Spring Boot code - Before committing Java changes

name: kotlin-review
description: Kotlin code review for coroutines, null safety, sealed classes, and architecture patterns. Invokes the kotlin-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: - After writing or modifying Kotlin code - Before committing Kotlin changes (Android or backend) - Reviewing pull requests with Kotlin code

name: learn-eval
description: Extract reusable patterns from the session, self-evaluate quality before saving, and determine the right save location (Global vs Project).
whenToUse: [Trigger conditions]

name: learning-audit
description: Audit the effectiveness of the clarc continuous-learning-v2 loop — instinct quality, conflict detector accuracy, evolution velocity, and learning coverage gaps. Produces a structured health report for the learning system.

name: migrate
description: Database migration workflow — plan, review, apply, and verify schema changes safely. Supports Drizzle, Prisma, Flyway, Alembic, golang-migrate. Guards against data-loss migrations reaching production.

name: mlops-review
description: MLOps audit — experiment tracking, model registry, serving SLOs, drift detection, retraining pipelines, A/B testing readiness. Invokes the mlops-architect agent.
whenToUse: Use /mlops-review when: - Deploying an ML model to production for the first time - Evaluating an existing ML system for operational gaps

name: mobile-release
description: Mobile release workflow — version bump, test, code signing, beta distribution (TestFlight / Firebase), and staged production rollout for iOS and Android.

name: modernize
description: Create a step-by-step modernization plan for a specific component — interface extraction, Branch-by-Abstraction, Strangler Fig, feature toggle cutover, and rollback strategy

name: multi-backend
description: [Requires codeagent-wrapper] Backend-focused multi-model workflow (Research→Ideation→Plan→Execute→Optimize→Review), Codex-led. Requires codeagent-wrapper + ace-tool MCP.

name: multi-execute
description: [Requires codeagent-wrapper] Multi-model collaborative execution — prototype from plan → Claude refactors and implements → multi-model audit and delivery.

name: multi-frontend
description: [Requires codeagent-wrapper] Frontend-focused multi-model workflow (Research→Ideation→Plan→Execute→Optimize→Review), Gemini-led. Requires codeagent-wrapper + ace-tool MCP.

name: multi-plan
description: [Requires codeagent-wrapper] Multi-model collaborative planning — Context retrieval + Codex/Gemini dual-model analysis → step-by-step implementation plan.

name: multi-workflow
description: [Requires codeagent-wrapper] Multi-model collaborative development workflow with intelligent routing — Frontend→Gemini, Backend→Codex. Full Research→Ideation→Plan→Execute→Optimize→Review cycle.

name: onboard
description: Generate onboarding documentation for an existing codebase — README, CONTRIBUTING.md, arc42 architecture docs, architecture tour, setup script validation, domain glossary, and GitHub onboarding issue template.

name: orchestrate
description: Orchestrate multiple agents for complex tasks — auto-selects coordination pattern (fan-out, split-role, explorer+validator, worktree, pipeline)

name: oss-setup
description: Set up open source project infrastructure for a GitHub repository — issue/PR templates, CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING.md, label taxonomy, and branch protection configuration.

name: overnight
description: Set up an autonomous overnight development pipeline. Analyzes the feature, selects the right pipeline pattern (Sequential, Continuous Claude, Ralphinho, or Infinite Loop), then generates all required files. WAITS for user confirmation before creating files.

name: php-review
description: PHP 8.4+ code review for strict types, security, Laravel/Symfony patterns, and PHPStan compliance. Invokes the php-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: - After writing or modifying PHP code - Before committing PHP changes - Reviewing pull requests with PHP code

name: plan
description: Restate requirements, assess risks, and create step-by-step implementation plan. WAIT for user CONFIRM before touching any code.
whenToUse: Use /plan when: - Starting a new feature - Making significant architectural changes

name: prd
description: Write a Product Requirements Document from a validated idea and its Architecture Decision Record. Produces a spec directly compatible with /overnight and /plan. Final step before implementation.

name: privacy-audit
description: Privacy Engineering audit — PII scan (Presidio), retention-policy check, data minimization, test-fixture scan, RTBF flow validation, consent-basis review, third-party data sharing.
whenToUse: Use /privacy-audit when: - Adding any new feature that collects personal data - Onboarding a new third-party service that receives user data

name: profile
description: Guided profiling workflow — detects language, recommends the right profiler, starts a session, and interprets the top hotspots with concrete next steps

name: project-init
description: Initialize a new project from a workflow starter-pack (rest-api, react-spa, python-pipeline, go-service, flutter-app, spring-boot)

name: promote
description: Promote project-scoped instincts to global scope

name: promote-skill
description: Promote a project-local skill from .clarc/skills/<name>/ to global clarc (~/.clarc/skills/). Optionally opens a PR to upstream clarc.

name: prompt-review
description: Review a system prompt or prompt template for quality, safety, and effectiveness. Invokes the prompt-reviewer agent.

name: python-review
description: Comprehensive Python code review for PEP 8 compliance, type hints, security, and Pythonic idioms. Invokes the python-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: Use /python-review when: - After writing or modifying Python code - Before committing Python changes

name: python-test
description: Run Python tests with coverage using pytest. Enforces 80%+ coverage and fails fast on the first error.
whenToUse: - Before committing Python changes - After adding new features (verify tests pass) - When CI reports failing tests

name: quickstart
description: Interactive onboarding for new clarc users. Guides through the first 5 minutes: explains the most important workflows, recommends which commands to start with, and gives a practical tour with concrete examples.

name: r-review
description: R code review for tidyverse idioms, tidy evaluation, Shiny security, and renv lockfile management. Invokes the r-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: - After writing or modifying R code - Before committing data analysis scripts or Shiny apps - Reviewing R packages for CRAN submission

name: refactor
description: Safely identify and remove dead code with test verification at every step.

name: release
description: Cut a new release. Reads git history since the last tag, determines the version bump (semver), updates CHANGELOG, bumps version files, creates a git tag, and generates GitHub Release notes.

name: resilience-audit
description: Systematic resilience audit of an architecture or codebase — inventories dependencies, checks failure handling, identifies SPOFs, outputs prioritized action plan

name: ruby-review
description: Comprehensive Ruby/Rails code review for idiomatic Ruby, Rails best practices, security (Brakeman), performance, and N+1 detection. Invokes the ruby-reviewer agent.

name: rules-diff
description: Preview what has changed in clarc rules since your installed version — without applying changes. Dry-run mode for /update-rules.

name: rust-build
description: Build Rust code and run Clippy lints. Catches compilation errors and lint warnings with minimal, surgical fixes.
whenToUse: - When cargo build fails with errors - When cargo clippy reports warnings - After pulling changes that break compilation

name: rust-review
description: Comprehensive Rust code review for ownership, borrowing, async correctness (Tokio), error handling (thiserror/anyhow), and performance. Invokes the rust-reviewer agent.

name: rust-test
description: Run Rust tests with coverage. Enforces TDD workflow with table-driven tests and 80%+ coverage via cargo test and cargo tarpaulin.

name: sbom
description: Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials), run vulnerability scan, and set up attestation with cosign — full workflow from generation to CI integration

name: scala-review
description: Scala code review for functional idioms, Cats Effect/ZIO patterns, ADT correctness, and null safety. Invokes the scala-reviewer agent.
whenToUse: - After writing or modifying Scala code - Before committing functional Scala changes - Reviewing Cats Effect or ZIO-based code

name: sdk-review
description: SDK Design Review — ergonomics, consistency, backward compatibility, error hierarchy, documentation coverage, multi-language generation, and VCR/contract test readiness. Invokes the sdk-architect agent.
whenToUse: Use /sdk-review when: - Releasing a new major or minor version of an SDK - Onboarding a new language into a multi-language SDK suite

name: security
description: Run comprehensive DevSecOps security scan — SAST (Semgrep), secrets (Gitleaks), dependencies (Trivy), container images, DAST (ZAP), and Policy-as-Code (OPA/Kyverno). Produces prioritized CRITICAL/HIGH report with fix plan.

name: session-cost
description: Show estimated token usage and cost for recent clarc sessions

name: sessions
description: Manage Claude Code session history — list, load, alias, and edit sessions stored in ~/.claude/sessions/.

name: setup-ci
description: Generate GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows for the current project. Detects language, test runner, and deployment target, then creates build/test/lint, Docker, and deploy workflows.

name: setup-dev
description: Set up a complete local development environment for a new project. Generates devcontainer.json, .env.example, docker-compose.dev.yml for services, and a Getting Started section in the README.

name: setup-pm
description: Configure your preferred package manager (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun)

name: skill-create
description: Analyze local git history to extract coding patterns and generate SKILL.md files. Local version of the Skill Creator GitHub App.
whenToUse: <describe the situation that calls for this skill>

name: skill-depth
description: Analyze a clarc skill for prompt-engineering quality — actionability ratio, trigger precision, example completeness, internal consistency, length calibration, cross-reference validity, and freshness. Use /skill-depth --all to analyze all skills.

name: skill-impact
description: Show which agents depend on a skill and what would be affected by changing it

name: skills-local
description: List all project-local skills in .clarc/skills/. Shows skill names, descriptions, and which ones override global clarc skills.

name: slide-deck
description: Generate a slide deck structure from a topic, document, or notes. Produces a Reveal.js HTML presentation or Markdown slide structure (Marp-compatible). Invokes the presentation-designer agent.

name: slo
description: Define SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets for a service. Produces a complete SLO document with burn rate alert rules and deployment gating policy.

name: storybook-audit
description: Audit an existing Storybook setup — story coverage, CSF version, accessibility, interaction tests, documentation quality, Chromatic integration, and prioritized improvement plan

name: swift-build
description: Build Swift/Xcode projects and run static analysis. Catches compilation errors, warnings, and analyzer issues.
whenToUse: - When swift build or xcodebuild fails - After pulling changes that break compilation - Before committing Swift changes

name: swift-review
description: Comprehensive Swift code review for concurrency safety, SwiftUI best practices, protocol-based architecture, memory management, and security. Invokes the swift-reviewer agent.

name: system-review
description: Orchestrate a structured self-review of the entire clarc system. Three modes: quick (wiring + naming, ~5 min), components (all component analyzers, ~30 min), full (everything + competitive analysis, ~90 min). Produces a report in docs/system-review/.

name: talk-outline
description: Create a talk outline with time allocation, key points per section, speaker notes skeleton, and Q&A preparation questions.

name: tdd
description: Enforce test-driven development workflow. Scaffold interfaces, generate tests FIRST, then implement minimal code to pass. Ensure 80%+ coverage.
whenToUse: Use /tdd when: - Implementing new features - Adding new functions/components

name: team-sync
description: Sync team clarc installation — pull latest clarc + private rules/skills, re-run team install

name: test-coverage
description: Analyze test coverage, identify gaps, and generate missing tests to reach 80%+ coverage.

name: triage
description: Run an issue triage session — list open issues, suggest labels, identify stale issues, and generate standard close responses for out-of-scope or duplicate issues.

name: typescript-build
description: Type-check and lint TypeScript/JavaScript code. Runs tsc --noEmit and ESLint/Biome to catch type errors and style violations without building.
whenToUse: - Before committing TypeScript changes - After pulling changes that may introduce type errors - When tsc or eslint is failing in CI

name: typescript-review
description: Comprehensive TypeScript code review for hexagonal architecture, DDD patterns, type safety, security, and performance. Invokes the typescript-reviewer agent.

name: undo
description: Undo last clarc auto-checkpoint (restores files to state before last Edit/Write)

name: update-codemaps
description: Analyze the codebase structure and generate token-lean architecture documentation in docs/CODEMAPS/.

name: update-docs
description: Sync documentation with the codebase, generating from source-of-truth files.

name: update-rules
description: Apply latest clarc rule updates from upstream. Skips user-modified files. Use --dry-run to preview, --force to overwrite user modifications.

name: verify
description: Run comprehensive build, type, lint, test, and git verification on the codebase.

name: visual-test
description: Set up visual regression testing for a project — detects project type, recommends tool (Chromatic/Playwright/Percy), installs, creates baselines, and configures CI

name: wasm-build
description: Build, optimize, and validate a WebAssembly module from Rust — wasm-pack build, wasm-opt size optimization, and JS integration check

name: wasm-review
description: Review WebAssembly/Rust code for correctness, performance pitfalls, unsafe memory management, and JS interop quality

name: web-perf
description: Web performance audit — run Lighthouse, interpret Core Web Vitals, analyze bundle, identify quick wins, and set up regression prevention

name: webrtc-review
description: WebRTC architecture review — P2P vs SFU vs MCU decision, TURN configuration, signaling robustness, simulcast, access token security, recording consent, and error handling.

name: whats-the-rule
description: Look up the rule constraint AND the linked skill for a given topic — makes the two-layer rules/skills system navigable

name: wireframe
description: Create a wireframe or user flow for a feature — generates ASCII/text wireframes, user flow diagrams, IA structure, and annotated specs. Use before writing UI code to validate structure and communicate intent.

name: workflow-check
description: Check whether clarc supports 6 critical end-to-end developer journeys — Idea-to-Production, Bug-to-Fix, New Developer Onboarding, Incident Response, Architecture Decision, and LLM App Development. Identifies which journey steps lack Command, Agent, or Skill coverage.

name: zero-trust-review
description: Zero-Trust security review for a service — mTLS enforcement, SPIFFE identity, NetworkPolicies, Authorization Policies, east-west traffic, and secret handling.
whenToUse: Use /zero-trust-review when: - Onboarding a new service to a service mesh (Istio, Linkerd) - Migrating from PERMISSIVE to STRICT mTLS mode
